Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user asks: You have just heard the long, detailed explanation above from an interviewee describing their property onboarding process. Your goal is to ask the interviewee a series of questions to clarify and improve your understanding of their process. The questions should aim to uncover missing details, understand decision criteria, clarify roles and responsibilities, verify timing and sequencing, and learn about how exceptions are handled. We need to generate a list of targeted, open-ended questions that the interviewer could ask the interviewee to clarify the property onboarding process.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key aspects: documentation verification, data entry, property manager assignment criteria, introductory meeting expectations, scheduling photographer and inspector, inspection issue resolution, marketing creation, activation in portal, tenant screening, lease finalization, quality checks and internal audits, exception handling (special insurance, custom lease clauses), communication flow and tracking.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We should ask about details missing: e.g., what are the exact criteria for assigning property manager? How are workload and specialization balanced? What is the timeline for each step? Who is responsible for contacting the landlord for missing documents? How are the property details recorded? What is the role of the regional office? What does the digital marketing team base pricing models on? How are promotional discounts decided? How are multiple interested tenants handled? Who negotiates lease terms? What happens if inspection reveals major issues? How is the decision made to postpone activation? How is the compliance officer involved? What are the internal audit criteria? What are the typical exceptions and how are they escalated? How is communication tracked? Are there any SLAs? What technologies are used?

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the answer will be a list of open-ended questions, grouped perhaps by categories: Documentation, Data Capture, Property Manager Assignment, Introductory Meeting, Inspection and Repairs, Marketing and Pricing, Tenant Screening and Selection, Lease Finalization, Quality Assurance and Compliance, Exception Handling, Communication and Tracking, Timing and SLAs, External Stakeholders.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Make sure not to request any SQL queries or implementation details, but rather seek to deepen your conceptual understanding of the process. We must ensure the questions are open-ended and not leading.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus answer will be something like: - How does the property coordinator decide which documents are mandatory versus optional? - What is the typical timeframe for landlords to provide missing documentation? - How is the central property database structured, and what fields are captured? - What criteria are used to match a property manager to a property? - How is workload measured and balanced across managers?

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We could include about communication, internal CRM usage, project management tools, tracking. And about exceptions: special insurance, custom lease clauses. We should also ask about stakeholder responsibilities: who does what at each step. Eg: Who schedules photographer and inspector? Who decides on repair contractors? Who approves marketing materials? Who performs tenant screening? Who makes final lease decision? Who does compliance review? Who does financial analysis? Who performs IT checks? Also about escalation: what happens if issues arise at any stage, who resolves them, what is the process. Also about timeline: what are typical durations for each step, any SLA. Also about handoffs: how are tasks transferred between teams, any sign-offs. Also about risk: any compliance risks, fair housing, data privacy.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus a comprehensive set of questions. Lets produce a structured answer. Will produce categories: Documentation & Verification, Data Capture & System Integration, Property Manager Assignment, Introductory Meeting & Expectation Alignment, Scheduling & Execution of Property Inspection & Photography, Repair & Issue Resolution, Marketing Creation & Pricing, Property Activation & Portal, Tenant Screening & Selection, Lease Finalization & Move-In, Quality Assurance & Internal Audits, Exception Handling & Escalations, Communication & Tracking, Timing & SLAs, External Stakeholder Management. Each category will have a few open-ended questions.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to ensure the questions are open-ended, not yes/no. We need to be careful not to request implementation details.
